aloof_plane_octopus_aspire: We are a student research group operating at the Institute of Computer Science. We are united by a passion for technology – from low-level theory to practical implementations.
inner_grand_sloth_aid: We operate socially, technically, and scientifically.
cuddly_born_skunk_wave: Either something broke, or you were looking for luck on our website. We assure you that the only luck is hidden in room 24 at the Institute of Computer Science!
direct_next_newt_relish: Feel free to visit us in your spare time, and now click here to return home.
true_mean_trout_hush: // Winter Camp of Computer Science Students
crazy_strong_antelope_climb: Winter Camp of Computer Science Students (ZOSIA) is an event organized by the Computer Science Students' Association for over 20 years. The history of the camp began with informal trips of a group of friends; today, it brings together nearly 300 people every year: students from various universities, representatives of academia, and IT companies.
dizzy_full_anaconda_nourish: During the three-day trip, there are lectures, company presentations, as well as excursions and integration parties. ZOSIA traditionally takes place at the turn of February and March, sharing a passion for technology in a unique winter atmosphere.
lucky_tired_seal_dine: Archaeologists are wondering if ZOSIA {year} even took place. No evidence found...
lower_sour_tiger_gaze: We don't have photos from this edition of ZOSIA. If you have any, you can send them to us so we can post them here.
zippy_steep_shell_dash: Can a Slime Mold train Neural Networks?
helpful_gaudy_cheetah_breathe: Visualization of U-Matrix, Hit Map, and Weight Vectors
giant_grand_mayfly_pout: This project explores the fascinating intersection of biology and computer science, testing whether the natural processes of <em class="italic text-base-content font-serif" >Physarum polycephalum</em > can be used to perform computational tasks, such as training neural networks.
spicy_dry_peacock_cure: Behavioral and experimental research on <em class="italic font-serif" >Physarum polycephalum</em >.
suave_many_squid_adore: Algorithmic analysis and modeling of transport networks.
wide_kind_jan_aim: Disseminating results within and beyond the academic community.

seemly_flaky_myna_buy: Institute of Computer Science, University of Wrocław
brave_fancy_owl_spin: The "Hour of Code" classes organized by the Computer Science Students' Club of the University of Wrocław introduce students to the Python programming language. They are intended for primary school students in grades 4-8. The classes consist of two parts:
eager_loud_owl_crawl: With the help of volunteers, computer science students, the pupils will independently solve programming tasks in computer labs.
dizzy_clever_dog_climb: The basics of programming in Python will be presented in an interactive, accessible, yet academic way.
shy_clever_tadpole_skip: We will start with a lecture where students will get acquainted with the theory: they will learn how to run the environment and write simple programs in Python, using standard input and output, basic data types, and conditional statements. Then they will learn about more advanced constructs like loops or functions. They will also have the opportunity to see interesting projects encouraging further learning.
shy_glad_apple_seek: After a break and a sweet treat, students will be able to put newly learned concepts into practice during the lab session by solving tasks independently. Volunteers will be present at the lab, ready to help the participants. The estimated total duration of the workshops is about 3 hours.
calm_glad_tadpole_dive: We will start with a lecture where students will get acquainted with the theory: they will learn how to run the environment and write simple programs in Python, using standard input and output, basic data types, and conditional statements. Then they will learn about more advanced constructs like loops or functions. They will also have the opportunity to see interesting projects encouraging further learning.
calm_brave_horse_walk: After a break and a sweet treat, students will be able to put newly learned concepts into practice during the lab session by solving tasks independently. Volunteers will be present at the lab, ready to help the participants. The estimated total duration of the workshops is about 3 hours.
calm_loud_owl_fly: Hour of Code is a chance for students to have their first encounter with and become fascinated by computer science. We try to conduct the classes in such a way that participants not only get to know a programming language, but also can develop their logical thinking and problem-solving skills.
clever_away_lion_roll: In 2026, the classes will take place on: April 10, April 24, May 4, and May 11. The classes are held in the lecture halls and computer labs of the Institute of Computer Science at the University of Wrocław, at 15 Fryderyka Joliota-Curie Street in Wrocław. We invite schools to register their willingness to participate in these classes by email at <span class="text-primary" >hourofcode.ksi [at] gmail.com</span >
cool_loud_apple_crawl: Hour of Code organized by the Computer Science Students' Club of the University of Wrocław is already a tradition. We present selected photos from previous editions of Hour of Code
cool_cool_mouse_climb: Teaching primary school students programming, presenting computer science as a field of work and study.
shy_calm_horse_swim: See images from Hour of Code 2026!
wild_jolly_lion_climb: Investigating the model's behavior depending on the training method.
happy_happy_apple_fly: Training a model that generates legal and strong chess moves resembling human play.
shy_glad_horse_skip: Krasnal's goal is to generate strong, yet human-like chess moves. Balancing between the intuition known from the Maia Chess project and the playing strength of Stockfish.
jolly_calm_lion_jump: Krasnal is a chess engine developed in Wrocław, based on the Transformer architecture. It utilizes deep neural networks to learn to play chess intuitively, departing from traditional, heavy calculations and game tree searching.
jolly_dizzy_dog_swim: Check out what we're working on!
